name: Hurley Vineyard
signature: Kevin Bell's boutique single-vineyard Pinot project — three tiny blocks (Lodestone, Garamond, Hommage) and some of the Peninsula's most sought-after bottles
editorNote:
Hurley is a serious wine person's cellar door. Kevin Bell's project is built around three tiny, meticulously farmed blocks  -  Lodestone, Garamond, and Hommage  -  and the Pinot Noirs that come out of them are allocation-only, routinely top the Peninsula line-ups in blind tastings, and age on the back of a vintage as well as anything in the region.

Tastings are by appointment and the experience is unhurried and conversational: Kevin or a senior team member pouring, walking you block by block, and explaining how a hundred metres of vineyard can change a wine's personality. Expect to buy; expect to wait; expect to learn more about Peninsula Pinot in an hour than you could read in a week of reviews.

This is the cellar door for people who already love Peninsula Pinot and want to understand what it can become when a single operator obsesses over the detail.

visiting.tastingNote: Walk-in every weekend 11am–5pm. No appointment required for weekend visits. No on-site restaurant.

editorVerdict: Hurley Vineyard is a conviction play. Kevin Bell planted in 1998, makes four distinct Pinot Noirs from the estate, uses no other variety, and ferments with indigenous yeast. The result is as site-specific as Peninsula wine gets — Balnarring terroir, nothing else. Serious Pinot drinkers should make the trip.

faq.q: Does Hurley Vineyard take walk-ins or do you need to book?
faq.a: Walk-ins welcome every weekend 11am–5pm. No appointment required.

faq.q: Does Hurley Vineyard have a restaurant?
Are dogs allowed at Hurley Vineyard?
faq.a: Dog policy not confirmed in official sources — check before visiting.
